label: 154 Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 96–591, § 8(a)94 Stat. 3435Dec. 24, 1980
notes: Section, acts , ; , ; , , provided for adoption of rules of navigation of harbors, rivers, and inland waters.June 7, 1897, ch. 4, § 130 Stat. 96May 21, 1948, ch. 328, § 162 Stat. 249Aug. 8, 1953, ch. 386, § 167 Stat. 497

CodificationPrior rules for preventing collision prescribed by R.S. § 4233 to be followed by vessels of the Navy and mercantile marine of the United States, applicable originally to all waters, were superseded as to navigation on the high seas and waters connected therewith by the International Rules ( [sec. 61 et seq. of this title]) were superseded as to navigation on the Great Lakes and their connecting and tributary waters as far east as Montreal, by (section 241 et seq. of this title); were adopted as special rules for the navigation of harbors, rivers, and inland waters of the United States, except the Great Lakes and their connecting and tributary waters as far east as Montreal by (see section 301 et seq. of this title); and were superseded by , as to navigation of all harbors, rivers, and inland waters of the United States except as specified in this paragraph, leaving them applicable solely to the Red River of the North and the rivers emptying into the Gulf of Mexico and their tributaries.act Aug. 19, 1890, ch. 802act Feb. 8, 1895, ch. 64act of Feb. 19, 1895, ch. 102act June 7, 1897, ch. 4
